title: poa compressa subsp. langiana
intro: Poa compressa subsp. langiana is a subspecies of the Poa compressa, commonly known as rough meadow-grass or smooth meadow-grass, which is a type of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The health benefits of Poa compressa subsp. langiana include potential anti-cancer, anti-diabetic, and cardiovascular protective effects, making it a subject of interest in modern herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a dried herb or in the form of a tea, infusion, or decoction, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
